If you notice an issue, please consider making an edit, starting a discussion on the article's talk page, or reaching out via the [Arknights Terra Wiki Discord](https://discord.gg/2eXU4KHYpB). # Operator From Arknights Terra Wiki [Jump to navigation](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#mw-head) [Jump to search](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#searchInput) | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Operator | [Alternates](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator/Alternate "Operator/Alternate") | [Free](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator/Free "Operator/Free") | [Special](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator/Special "Operator/Special") | [Limited](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator/Limited "Operator/Limited") | [Exclusive](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator/Exclusive "Operator/Exclusive") | **Operators** are the playable [characters](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Character "Character") in _[Arknights](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Arknights "Arknights")_. Acting as the "towers" in _Arknights's_ tower defense setting, Operators are placed in locations around the battlefield to engage enemies and keep them from reaching the objective. Operators come in six **rarities** determining their overall performance, denoted by a number of stars from 1 to 6. ## Contents - [1List](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#List) - [1.1New & upcoming](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#New_&_upcoming) - [2Overview](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Overview) - [2.1Squads](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Squads) - [2.2Classes](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Classes) - [2.3Attributes](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Attributes) - [2.4Acquisition](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Acquisition) - [2.5Roles](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Roles) - [3Trivia](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Trivia) - [4Navigation](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator#Navigation) ## List\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=1 "Edit section: List")\] Currently the Global server has 401 out of 418 Operators available on the CN server. _For a full list of all released and upcoming Operators, see [Operator/List](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator/List "Operator/List")_. [![Zinogre S Catapult](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Zinogre_S_Catapult_icon.png/60px-Zinogre_S_Catapult_icon.png?e012c8)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Zinogre_S_Catapult) | ### New & upcoming\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=2 "Edit section: New & upcoming")\] | Server | Operator(s) | Introduced in (date) | | --- | --- | --- | | **Global** | [![Perfumer the Distilled](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Perfumer_the_Distilled_icon.png/60px-Perfumer_the_Distilled_icon.png?b036c7)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Perfumer_the_Distilled)

[![Snow Hunter](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Snow_Hunter_icon.png/60px-Snow_Hunter_icon.png?defc2a)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Snow_Hunter) | [Retracing Our Steps](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Retracing_Our_Steps "Retracing Our Steps")
(2026/06/10) | ## Overview\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=3 "Edit section: Overview")\] Operators have many functionalities, ranging from directly fighting the enemy either with melee or long-ranged weaponry, using [Originium Arts](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Originium_Arts "Originium Arts") to attack the enemy with destructive magic, healing other Operators, or providing some form of offensive or defensive support. There are two types of Operators: melee and ranged. Melee Operators are deployed on low ground tiles while ranged Operators are deployed on high ground tiles, although certain Operators can be deployed in both low and high ground tiles, and certain operations allow all Operators to be deployed on low ground tiles. Most Operators have at least one and at most three [skills](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Skill "Skill") to use, up to two [base skills](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Base_skill "Base skill"), and various [talents](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Talent "Talent"). The majority of Operators are aligned with [Rhodes Island](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Rhodes_Island "Rhodes Island"), although some of them act as contacts or liaisons of their [faction](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Faction "Faction"), mercenaries, or volunteers. This does not affect Operators' accessibility and usage during gameplay, however. Specific Operators also have their own [Operator Records](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator_Record "Operator Record") and [Paradox Simulation](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Paradox_Simulation "Paradox Simulation"). Refer to the respective articles for details. ### Squads\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=4 "Edit section: Squads")\] Operators are assigned into **squads**, with a player having four squads to assign their Operators into that can be renamed to their liking. Outside of [Integrated Strategies](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Integrated_Strategies "Integrated Strategies") and [Stationary Security Service](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Stationary_Security_Service "Stationary Security Service"), a squad is made up of 12 Operators. As Operators are unique, _only one of each Operator may be in a squad at a time._ However, the [alternate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Alternate_Operator "Alternate Operator") version of an Operator _can_ be present alongside the original Operator in a squad. ### Classes\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=5 "Edit section: Classes")\] _Main article: [Class](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Class "Class")_ ### Attributes\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=6 "Edit section: Attributes")\] _Main article: [Attribute](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Attribute "Attribute")_ Operators' attributes are displayed on the left side of the Character Details page, above the character name. As the Operator gain levels, their attributes will be increased. The attribute cap will be increased when the Operator is [promoted](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Promotion "Promotion"). Increasing the Operator's potential and [Trust](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Trust "Trust") will also provide a bonus to their attributes. ### Acquisition\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=7 "Edit section: Acquisition")\] There are two main ways of obtaining Operators: [headhunting](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Headhunting "Headhunting") and [recruitment](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Recruitment "Recruitment"). Certain [6★](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/6-star "6-star") and [5★](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/5-star "5-star") Operators are available for purchase in the [Distinctions Store](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Distinctions_Store "Distinctions Store") for 180 [![Distinction Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Distinction_Certificate_icon.png/20px-Distinction_Certificate_icon.png?c028a0)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Distinction_Certificate "Distinction Certificate") and 45 [![Distinction Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Distinction_Certificate_icon.png/20px-Distinction_Certificate_icon.png?c028a0)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Distinction_Certificate "Distinction Certificate"), respectively; the available Operators differ in every standard and Kernel [headhunting banner](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Headhunting/Banners "Headhunting/Banners") rotation. - The Operators from the current Kernel headhunting banner may also be purchased for 1800 [![Universal Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Universal_Certificate_icon.png/20px-Universal_Certificate_icon.png?91091c)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Universal_Certificate "Universal Certificate") and 450 [![Universal Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Universal_Certificate_icon.png/20px-Universal_Certificate_icon.png?91091c)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Universal_Certificate "Universal Certificate"), respectively. [Courier](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Courier), [Dur-nar](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Dur-nar), and [Gavial](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Gavial) can be purchased at the [Credit Store](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Credit_Store "Credit Store"). [Ethan](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Ethan), [Pudding](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Pudding), [Quartz](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Quartz), [Caper](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Caper) and [Contrail](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Contrail) can be purchased at the [Purchase Certificate Store](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Purchase_Certificate_Store "Purchase Certificate Store") for 150 [![Purchase Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Purchase_Certificate_icon.png/20px-Purchase_Certificate_icon.png?e78de6)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Purchase_Certificate "Purchase Certificate"). [Qanipalaat](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Qanipalaat), [Breeze](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Breeze), [Honeyberry](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Honeyberry), [Diamante](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Diamante) and [Philae](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Philae) are also available for 600 [![Purchase Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Purchase_Certificate_icon.png/20px-Purchase_Certificate_icon.png?e78de6)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Purchase_Certificate "Purchase Certificate"). Obtaining duplicates of Operators the player already own will give the respective Operator's [Operator Token](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator_Token "Operator Token") instead, which can be used to improve the Operator's [Potential](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Potential "Potential") and traded for [![Commendation Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Commendation_Certificate.png/21px-Commendation_Certificate.png?6658a1)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Commendation_Certificate)[Commendation Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Commendation_Certificate) or [![Distinction Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Distinction_Certificate.png/21px-Distinction_Certificate.png?d81300)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Distinction_Certificate)[Distinction Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Distinction_Certificate) (or [![Universal Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/images/thumb/Universal_Certificate.png/21px-Universal_Certificate.png?108b7c)](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Universal_Certificate)[Universal Certificate](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Universal_Certificate), if the token was obtained from a Kernel Headhunting banner) once the corresponding Operator's potential is maxed out. ### Roles\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=8 "Edit section: Roles")\] Operators are defined by their **roles** or **specializations** that they are suited to be used as a combination of their class, attributes, trait, skills, and talents. | Role | Description | | --- | --- | | **AOE** | Deals damage towards multiple targets, hence the name which stands for "area-of-effect". | | **Crowd-Control** | Limits the enemy's actions or disable them outright, usually by inflicting [Negative Statuses](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Negative_Status "Negative Status"). | | **Debuff** | Weaken enemies by inflicting various [debuffs](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Debuff "Debuff") on them, hence the name. | | **Defense** | Withstand attacks or hold off enemies. All [Defenders](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Defender "Defender") beside [Noir Corne](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Noir_Corne "Noir Corne") innately specialize in Defense. | | **DP-Recovery** | Generates additional [DP](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/DP "DP") to facilitate unit deployment. All [Vanguards](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Vanguard "Vanguard") beside [Yato](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Yato "Yato") innately specialize in DP-Recovery. | | **DPS** | Deals damage at a sustained rate, hence the name which stands for "damage per second". | | **Elemental** | Have abilities related to [Elemental Injury](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Elemental_Injury "Elemental Injury"), either by inflicting them on enemies or interacting with them. The [Ritualist Supporter](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Ritualist_Supporter "Ritualist Supporter"), [Primal Caster](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Primal_Caster "Primal Caster"), [Primal Protector Defender](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Primal_Protector_Defender "Primal Protector Defender"), and [Primal Guard](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Primal_Guard "Primal Guard") branches innately specialize in Elemental. | | **Fast-Redeploy** | Can be easily relocated due to their shorter redeployment time than other Operators. [Executors](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Executor_Specialist "Executor Specialist"), [Merchant Specialists](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Merchant_Specialist "Merchant Specialist"), and [Agent Vanguards](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Agent_Vanguard "Agent Vanguard") innately specialize in Fast-Redeploy. All [Dollkeepers](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Dollkeeper_Specialist "Dollkeeper Specialist") also have the Fast-Redeploy tag although they do not have a shorter redeployment time. | | **Healing** | Restores the HP of friendly units. All [Medics](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Medic "Medic") and [Guardian Defenders](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Guardian_Defender "Guardian Defender") innately specialize in Healing. | | **Nuker** | Deals a large amount of damage over a short timeframe (i.e. "burst damage"), usually towards multiple targets. | | **[Shift](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Shift "Shift")** | Displace enemies through the namesake mechanic. [Push Stroker](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Push_Stroker_Specialist "Push Stroker Specialist") and [Hookmaster Specialists](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Hookmaster_Specialist "Hookmaster Specialist") are innately specialized in Shift. | | **Slow** | Hinders the mobility of enemies. [Decel Binder Supporters](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Decel_Binder_Supporter "Decel Binder Supporter") innately specialize in Slow. | | **Soar** | Have abilities that allow them to block [aerial enemies](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Enemy#Aerial "Enemy") through the [Take Off](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Take_Off "Take Off") effect. [Skyranger Specialists](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Skyranger_Specialist "Skyranger Specialist") innately specialize in Soar. | | **[Summon](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Summon "Summon")** | Utilizes the namesake to act as force multiplier. [Summoner Supporters](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Summoner_Supporter "Summoner Supporter") other than [Magallan](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Magallan "Magallan") innately specialize in Summon. | | **Support** | Provides offensive and/or defensive backup for friendly units, allowing them to gain an advantage over enemies. | | **Survival** | Can last longer on the field than other Operators, allowing them to hold back enemies on their own. | ## Trivia\[ [edit](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Operator?action=edit§ion=9 "Edit section: Trivia")\] - After _The Wheat and the Anthem_ [headhunting banner](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Headhunting_banner "Headhunting banner"), the number of new 4★ Operators have significantly declined; with exceptions to collaboration/crossover events and Purchase Certificate Store, all 4★ Operators after that point, with the exception of [Lutonada](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Lutonada "Lutonada") who is released alongside the [Babel event](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Babel_(event) "Babel (event)"), are introduced only in headhunting banners debuting alongside a new Main Theme episode. - In the beta version of _Arknights_, all Operators' rarity were one star lower than what they were in the final release (e.g. [Exusiai](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Exusiai "Exusiai") would be a 5★ instead of the 6★). - Interestingly, a majority of the male 6★ Operators released thus far notably have an unusual trend of being heavily skewed towards being a [Guard](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Guard "Guard") or [Specialist](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Specialist "Specialist"), whereas all of the male 5★ or lower Operators and female Operators are far more varied in regards to their class. - The only few examples of male 6★ Operators who do not follow the above trend are operators such as [Gnosis](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Gnosis "Gnosis"), [Lumen](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Lumen "Lumen"), [Vigil](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Vigil "Vigil") and [Yu](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Yu "Yu"), whom have the distinction of being the first male 6★ [Supporter](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Supporter "Supporter"), [Medic](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Medic "Medic"), [Vanguard](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Vanguard "Vanguard"), and [Defender](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Defender "Defender") respectively. - As of January 22, 2025, there are no male 6★ [Snipers](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Sniper "Sniper") (not counting [Stormeye](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Stormeye "Stormeye") in [Stronghold Protocol](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Stronghold_Protocol "Stronghold Protocol")). - As of December 11, 2024, there are more 5★ or lower Operators with alternates than 6★ ones. ## Navigation Expand[View or edit this template](https://arknights.wiki.gg/wiki/Template:Features "Template:Features") _Arknights_ features Gameplay - metadata.og:title: Operator
metadata.description:
Operators are the playable characters in Arknights. Acting as the "towers" in Arknights's tower defense setting, Operators are placed in locations around the battlefield to engage enemies and keep them from reaching the objective.
Operators come in six rarities determining their overall performance,

metadata.ogDescription:
Operators are the playable characters in Arknights. Acting as the "towers" in Arknights's tower defense setting, Operators are placed in locations around the battlefield to engage enemies and keep them from reaching the objective.
Operators come in six rarities determining their overall performance,
